description: Between head and shoulders, 'nolue' is the external body region that connects them. Breath and food pass through it, as do blood vessels and nerves, but the word covers the region rather than the internal throat.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and move into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and raise the back of the tongue into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Move forward and unround into middle-height 'e' for the separate final syllable.
usage_notes: A more exact account of an internal passage needs its own anatomical term or preserved source terminology rather than stretching this regional noun.

semantic_domains.physical: the external region joining the head to the torso
